mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points in a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering negative equity returns over the next quarter as earnings expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE spikes > 0.5pp, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to generate positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals immediate supply-side bottlenecks and inventory drawdowns, causing short-term equity sell-offs over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are non-negative when INDPRO crosses below its 12-month MA, or predictive accuracy drops below 52% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with a rising unemployment rate creates a stagflationary environment that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, suppressing equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of stagflationary trigger months, or the interaction effect reverses sign in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP directly translates to higher corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as markets price in economic expansion.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows no statistical correlation with subsequent 20-day SPX returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden drop in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 100,000 in a single month indicates severe economic weakness, triggering immediate risk-off flows and negative equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of months following a >100k payroll drop, or the signal loses predictive power when controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling housing starts signals a manufacturing-led expansion that outpaces residential demand, supporting equity markets through improved corporate margins over the next month.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-HOUST divergence fails to predict positive 20-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction term becomes insignificant after adjusting for monetary policy shocks.
